_note: JSON sidecar for migration-manifest.yaml. The wizard runtime CLI consumes JSON; YAML is the human-facing companion. Both files MUST stay in sync.

artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/verification_m: Adds the verification-mode contract — defines how a check's result is classified (machine-verified, operator-attested, or unverifiable-locally) so every "done / correct" statement carries its method of confirmation.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/contracts.py: Defines the integrity-contract data structure binding an external write to its authority check, boundary proof, and recovery proof.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/verifiers.py: Implements the independent-route verifier — confirms an external write's effect through a declared route that is separate from the write path itself.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/boundary.py: Enforces the write-boundary contract — checks that a write stays within its declared scope and that the scope survives ordinary use.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/proof_hash.py: Produces and verifies copy-run proofs — content-addressed records that a new write was run on a copy, applied, then undone with the result independently confirmed.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/copy_run_proof: Orchestrates the full copy-run proof sequence (copy, apply, confirm, undo, confirm restoration) and records the proof artifact.
artifact_notes.operating_discipline.md: Step 5 sharpened — checks are now independent-route by requirement; contradictions from the operator are treated as correct until independently disproven; no absolute claims allowed on a fresh mechanism without an independent check.
artifact_notes.build_progress.md: Layer-B acceptance for external-write phases now requires a recorded copy-run proof before a phase can be marked accepted.

migrations.migration_steps: 1. Run the update check (it reports what's new + the recommendation). 2. On the operator's yes, the assistant applies it ("${WIZARD_HOME:-$HOME/agent-wizard}/scripts/wizard" self-upgrade --to v0.9.0 --apply). 3. A backup is taken first; the operator's own data, rules, credentials, and logs are never touched.
migrations.preflight: Verify the operator project carries a .wizard/manifest.json whose foundation_bundle_version is v0.8.0 (or any version in the supported range). A backup is taken automatically.
migrations.rollback: Restore from .wizard/backups/pre-v0.9.0/ and set foundation_bundle_version back to v0.8.0.
migrations.notes: Minor-additive release to v0.9.0: adds the External Data Change Integrity Contract — six new lib modules (verification_modes, contracts, verifiers, boundary, proof_hash, copy_run_proof) that give external writes an independent-check, copy-first proof, and proven undo. Operating-discipline prose updated with independent-check and pushback rules. No breaking changes; all prior operator project files remain valid.
